inspected_sources.evidence: Compose wrappers are the default runtime path. `npm run compose:build` then `npm run compose:up` is the preferred start sequence, `npm run compose` is the supported shortcut, and `npm run compose:down` is the stop path. The agent must not run `*:local:*` commands.

Documents the normal manual-proof frontend surfaces for the main stack and the fake-scenario e2e stack. It also states that the main and e2e stacks remain image-baked, while the local compose overlay live-mounts only `codex`, `codex_agents`, `flows`, and `flows-sandbox`.
Defines the supported main, local-overlay, and e2e compose wrapper commands used to build, start, and stop the runnable systems.
Defines the main full-system runtime: client on 5001, server on 5010/5011/5012, Mongo on 27517, Chroma on 8000, and the repo-provided Playwright MCP service on 8932.
Defines the supported local compose overlay runtime on 5501/5510/5511/5512/8931 and shows the bind-mounted runtime trees.
Defines the fake-scenario e2e compose runtime: client on 6001 and server on 6010/6011/6012.
Current main-stack overrides set `CODEINFO_CODEX_WORKDIR=${HOME}/code` and `CODEINFO_HOST_INGEST_DIR=${CODEINFO_CODEX_WORKDIR}`, so the ingest-visible host bind mount resolves to `/home/d_a_s/code`.
The wrapper owns env-file interpolation and auto-creates missing optional `.env.local` files before compose startup.
The server runtime starts headless Chromium with remote debugging on port 9222.
The server image exposes the HTTP server port and the Chrome DevTools port used by the compose-backed runtime.

repositories.notes: Only the current repository is in scope from the stored handoff. The checked-in main compose stack is the preferred full normal system for later manual proof.

alternate_supported_variants.name: local_live_mount_compose_overlay

freshness.proof: README only documents immediate visibility for the listed bind-mounted runtime trees. No broader freshness marker is documented, and AGENTS.md says this agent must not run `*:local:*` commands.

prerequisites: `server/.env`, `server/.env.local`, `client/.env`, and `client/.env.local` exist or can be auto-created by the compose wrapper.
The repo-local bind mount targets such as `./codex`, `./copilot`, `./codex_agents`, `./flows`, and `./flows-sandbox` are available.
alternate_supported_variants.notes: Supported for local development, but not the preferred proof path for this flow and not a runtime this agent should start under AGENTS.md.

surfaces.name: main_compose_stack

freshness.proof: README says the main stack remains image-baked, so an already-running stack is not safely provable as current after relevant changes. Rebuild and restart with the supported compose wrappers after those changes. When the full Story 50 validation flow is used, `DEV-0000050:T10:image_runtime_assets_baked`, `DEV-0000050:T11:host_network_runtime_ready`, and `DEV-0000050:T12:main_stack_probe_completed` are the documented validation markers.

prerequisites: Docker runtime is available and supports the repo's compose workflow.
`host.docker.internal` resolves from the host and compose-backed containers.
`server/.env`, `server/.env.local`, `client/.env`, and `client/.env.local` exist; the compose wrapper can auto-create missing optional `.env.local` files.
The current main-stack ingest bind mount resolves from `server/.env.local` as host `/home/d_a_s/code` to container `${CODEINFO_CODEX_WORKDIR}`.
Host Codex auth at `${CODEINFO_HOST_CODEX_HOME:-$HOME/.codex}` is needed only for auth-dependent Codex proof paths.
surfaces.notes: Preferred launcher for later Story 55 manual proof. Starts the normal client, server, Mongo, Chroma, observability services, and the repo-provided Playwright MCP service together.

surfaces.name: frontend_ui_main
surfaces.variant: browser UI from the main compose stack

freshness.proof: This UI is served from the image-baked main compose stack. No separate already-running freshness marker is documented beyond the main stack guidance.

prerequisites: The main compose stack is running.
surfaces.notes: Use this paired frontend for browser proof of Story 55 behavior on the normal system.

surfaces.name: server_api_and_browser_tooling_main
surfaces.variant: main compose server API plus connected browser tooling

restart_required_for: client code changes that affect paired browser proof
freshness.proof: This API and tooling surface is served from the image-baked main compose stack. No standalone freshness marker is documented for reusing an already-running server after relevant changes.

prerequisites: The main compose stack is running.
surfaces.notes: Use this paired backend/tooling surface when proof needs the live API, the Agents MCP server, the repo-provided Playwright MCP endpoint, or the server-owned Chrome DevTools endpoint.

surfaces.name: e2e_fake_scenario_stack

freshness.proof: README says the e2e stack remains image-baked, so an already-running e2e stack is not safely provable as current after relevant changes. Rebuild and restart with the supported e2e compose wrappers after those changes. The broader Story 50 validation flow also records `DEV-0000050:T13:e2e_host_network_config_verified` when that full e2e path is rerun.

prerequisites: If images are not already present, the repo provides `npm run compose:e2e:build` as the supported prebuild path.
surfaces.notes: Supported fake-scenario runtime for browser proof when a scenario-controlled stack is explicitly needed. This is not the default full-system path.

surfaces.name: frontend_ui_e2e
surfaces.variant: browser UI from the fake-scenario e2e stack

restart_required_for: server code changes that affect paired browser proof
freshness.proof: This UI is served from the image-baked fake-scenario e2e stack. No separate already-running freshness marker is documented beyond the e2e stack guidance.

prerequisites: The e2e fake-scenario stack is running.
surfaces.notes: Use this connected surface only when later manual proof explicitly needs the repository's fake-scenario browser runtime.
